blocks.original_text: The oldest were men grown, seventeen and eighteen years from the day of their naming. One was past twenty. Most were younger, sixteen or less.
blocks.explanation_zh: Winterfell新征守卫多为十六岁以下少年，少数十七至二十岁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 开头年龄表不是抽象人口统计，而是Winterfell守军被抽空后的replacement pool。

blocks.original_text: Bran watched them from the balcony of Maester Luwin’s turret, listening to them grunt and strain and curse as they swung their staves and wooden swords. The yard was alive to the clack of wood on wood, punctuated all too often by thwacks and yowls of pain when a blow struck leather or flesh. Ser Rodrik strode among the boys, face reddening beneath his white whiskers, muttering at them one and all. Bran had never seen the old knight look so fierce. “No,” he kept saying. “No. No. No.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran从Luwin turret看Ser Rodrik严训木剑与staves，yard充满撞击和疼叫。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Winterfell守军被抽空，Bran怨失双腿并向故事寻找战斗可能；三-eyed crow dream引他要求验证Eddard是否在crypt。

blocks.original_text: “They don’t fight very well,” Bran said dubiously. He scratched Summer idly behind the ears as the direwolf tore at a haunch of meat. Bones crunched between his teeth.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran判断他们打得很差，同时Summer咬碎肉骨。

blocks.original_text: “For a certainty,” Maester Luwin agreed with a deep sigh. The maester was peering through his big Myrish lens tube, measuring shadows and noting the position of the comet that hung low in the morning sky. “Yet given time . . . Ser Rodrik has the truth of it, we need men to walk the walls. Your lord father took the cream of his guard to King’s Landing, and your brother took the rest, along with all the likely lads for leagues around. Many will not come back to us, and we must needs find the men to take their places.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin一边测comet一边同意，却说Eddard和Robb带走精锐，必须训练替补守墙。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Luwin同时观察comet与训练yard，连接long-term knowledge和眼前防务；many will not return是现实预估。
blocks.background_zh: **Winterfell manpower：** Eddard带走household guard，Robb再带剩余适龄men南征，城堡正训练少年补缺。

blocks.original_text: Bran stared resentfully at the sweating boys below. “If I still had my legs, I could beat them all.” He remembered the last time he’d held a sword in his hand, when the king had come to Winterfell. It was only a wooden sword, yet he’d knocked Prince Tommen down half a hundred times. “Ser Rodrik should teach me to use a poleaxe. If I had a poleaxe with a big long haft, Hodor could be my legs. We could be a knight together.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran怨恨失去双腿，幻想与Hodor组成一名使用long-haft poleaxe的knight。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Hodor as legs表现Bran拒绝把disability等同无能，也仍用knighthood衡量价值。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: “I think that . . . unlikely,” Maester Luwin said. “Bran, when a man fights, his arms and legs and thoughts must be as one.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin说fight要求arms、legs与thoughts合一，这一方案不现实。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Luwin的身体整合判断务实，却未提供替代角色，容易让put dreams aside成为纯否定。

blocks.original_text: Below in the yard, Ser Rodrik was yelling. “You fight like a goose. He pecks you and you peck him harder. Parry! Block the blow. Goose fighting will not suffice. If those were real swords, the first peck would take your arm off!” One of the other boys laughed, and the old knight rounded on him. “You laugh. You. Now that is gall. You fight like a hedgehog . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Rodrik用goose peck和hedgehog比喻纠正boys不会parry的错误。

blocks.original_text: “There was a knight once who couldn’t see,” Bran said stubbornly, as Ser Rodrik went on below. “Old Nan told me about him. He had a long staff with blades at both ends and he could spin it in his hands and chop two men at once.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran以Old Nan故事中blind knight反驳身体障碍。

blocks.original_text: “Symeon Star-Eyes,” Luwin said as he marked numbers in a book. “When he lost his eyes, he put star sapphires in the empty sockets, or so the singers claim. Bran, that is only a story, like the tales of Florian the Fool. A fable from the Age of Heroes.” The maester tsked. “You must put these dreams aside, they will only break your heart.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin认出Symeon Star-Eyes，称其为Age of Heroes fable，劝Bran放下会伤心的dreams。
blocks.literary_note_zh: fable与dream在本章持续争论：Luwin以可验证性保护Bran，代价是可能忽略重复异常。
blocks.background_zh: **Symeon Star-Eyes：** Age of Heroes传奇blind knight，眼窝嵌star sapphires、持双头staff；Luwin视为fable。

blocks.original_text: The mention of dreams reminded him. “I dreamed about the crow again last night. The one with three eyes. He flew into my bedchamber and told me to come with him, so I did. We went down to the crypts. Father was there, and we talked. He was sad.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 提到dreams使Bran说三-eyed crow昨夜带他下crypts，Eddard在那里且很悲伤。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 三-eyed crow dream明确把Eddard、crypt和Jon相连，但具体Jon含义仍不明。
blocks.background_zh: **three-eyed crow：** Bran反复梦见的引导者；本次带他到Eddard crypt并谈及Jon，含义未解。

blocks.original_text: “And why was that?” Luwin peered through his tube.

blocks.original_text: “It was something to do about Jon, I think.” The dream had been deeply disturbing, more so than any of the other crow dreams. “Hodor won’t go down into the crypts.”

blocks.original_text: “Go down into the crypts. When I woke, I told him to take me down, to see if Father was truly there. At first he didn’t know what I was saying, but I got him to the steps by telling him to go here and go there, only then he wouldn’t go down. He just stood on the top step and said ‘Hodor,’ like he was scared of the dark, but I had a torch. It made me so mad I almost gave him a swat in the head, like Old Nan is always doing.” He saw the way the maester was frowning and hurriedly added, “I didn’t, though.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran解释自己想验证Eddard是否真在，Hodor到stairs却恐惧拒绝；他虽生气但没有打Hodor。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bran差点打Hodor显示frustration如何可能向更弱者转移；补充I didn’t是道德自检。

blocks.original_text: The maester tugged at the chain around his neck, as he often did when he was uncomfortable. “Bran, sweet child, one day Lord Eddard will sit below in stone, beside his father and his father’s father and all the Starks back to the old Kings in the North . . . but that will not be for many years, gods be good. Your father is a prisoner of the queen in King’s Landing. You will not find him in the crypts.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin不安地说Eddard未来终会葬入Stark crypt，但现在只是Cersei prisoner，不会在那里。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Luwin正确陈述现有political information，却把梦与现实互斥得过早。

blocks.original_text: In place of Hodor, the wildling woman Osha was summoned. She was tall and tough and uncomplaining, willing to go wherever she was commanded. “I lived my life beyond the Wall, a hole in the ground won’t fret me none, m’lords,” she said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Osha被召来；她说Wall外生活使地下洞穴不足以吓她。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha既是被命令servant也保留wildling视角；ankle shackles显示信任仍有限。
blocks.background_zh: **Osha status：** wildling prisoner因服务可靠被去掉主chain，仍戴ankle shackles，介于servant与detainee。

blocks.original_text: “Summer, come,” Bran called as she lifted him in wiry-strong arms. The direwolf left his bone and followed as Osha carried Bran across the yard and down the spiral steps to the cold vault under the earth. Maester Luwin went ahead with a torch. Bran did not even mind—too badly—that she carried him in her arms and not on her back. Ser Rodrik had ordered Osha’s chain struck off, since she had served faithfully and well since she had been at Winterfell. She still wore the heavy iron shackles around her ankles—a sign that she was not yet wholly trusted—but they did not hinder her sure strides down the steps.
blocks.explanation_zh: Osha抱Bran下crypt，Summer跟随，Luwin举torch；Osha已去掉主chain但仍戴ankle shackles。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha带Bran入crypt，Stark history与empty tombs展开；Shaggydog袭Luwin，Rickon也说梦见Eddard。

blocks.original_text: Bran could not recall the last time he had been in the crypts. It had been before, for certain. When he was little, he used to play down here with Robb and Jon and his sisters.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran记不清上次来crypts，肯定是在坠落之前。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha带Bran入crypt，Stark history与empty tombs展开；Shaggydog袭Luwin，Rickon也说梦见Eddard。

blocks.original_text: He wished they were here now; the vault might not have seemed so dark and scary. Summer stalked out in the echoing gloom, then stopped, lifted his head, and sniffed the chill dead air. He bared his teeth and crept backward, eyes glowing golden in the light of the maester’s torch. Even Osha, hard as old iron, seemed uncomfortable. “Grim folk, by the look of them,” she said as she eyed the long row of granite Starks on their stone thrones.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他思念曾一起玩耍的siblings；Summer和Osha都对stone Starks与dead air不安。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Summer refusal不是语言证明，却与Hodor、Osha不安形成跨角色警报。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: “They were the Kings of Winter,” Bran whispered. Somehow it felt wrong to talk too loudly in this place.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran低声称他们是Kings of Winter。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha带Bran入crypt，Stark history与empty tombs展开；Shaggydog袭Luwin，Rickon也说梦见Eddard。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: Osha smiled. “Winter’s got no king. If you’d seen it, you’d know that, summer boy.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Osha说真正winter没有king，称他summer boy。
blocks.literary_note_zh: winter has no king纠正title傲慢，也暗含Osha亲历的winter不同于Bran故事。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: “They were the Kings in the North for thousands of years,” Maester Luwin said, lifting the torch high so the light shone on the stone faces. Some were hairy and bearded, shaggy men fierce as the wolves that crouched by their feet. Others were shaved clean, their features gaunt and sharp-edged as the iron longswords across their laps. “Hard men for a hard time. Come.” He strode briskly down the vault, past the procession of stone pillars and the endless carved figures. A tongue of flame trailed back from the upraised torch as he went.
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin解释Kings in North是hard times的hard men，举火经过无尽statues。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha带Bran入crypt，Stark history与empty tombs展开；Shaggydog袭Luwin，Rickon也说梦见Eddard。
blocks.background_zh: **Kings in the North：** Stark ancestors在Aegon conquest前统治North；Torrhen kneel后改为Lords of Winterfell。

blocks.original_text: The vault was cavernous, longer than Winterfell itself, and Jon had told him once that there were other levels underneath, vaults even deeper and darker where the older kings were buried. It would not do to lose the light. Summer refused to move from the steps, even when Osha followed the torch, Bran in her arms.
blocks.explanation_zh: vault比Winterfell更长且有更深层；Summer拒绝离开steps，凸显动物警觉。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha带Bran入crypt，Stark history与empty tombs展开；Shaggydog袭Luwin，Rickon也说梦见Eddard。

blocks.original_text: “Do you recall your history, Bran?” the maester said as they walked. “Tell Osha who they were and what they did, if you can.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin让Bran向Osha复述Stark history。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha带Bran入crypt，Stark history与empty tombs展开；Shaggydog袭Luwin，Rickon也说梦见Eddard。

blocks.original_text: He looked at the passing faces and the tales came back to him. The maester had told him the stories, and Old Nan had made them come alive. “That one is Jon Stark. When the sea raiders landed in the east, he drove them out and built the castle at White Harbor. His son was Rickard Stark, not my father’s father but another Rickard, he took the Neck away from the Marsh King and married his daughter. Theon Stark’s the real thin one with the long hair and the skinny beard. They called him the ‘Hungry Wolf,’ because he was always at war. That’s a Brandon, the tall one with the dreamy face, he was Brandon the Shipwright, because he loved the sea. His tomb is empty. He tried to sail west across the Sunset Sea and was never seen again. His son was Brandon the Burner, because he put the torch to all his father’s ships in grief. There’s Rodrik Stark, who won Bear Island in a wrestling match and gave it to the Mormonts. And that’s Torrhen Stark, the King Who Knelt. He was the last King in the North and the first Lord of Winterfell, after he yielded to Aegon the Conqueror. Oh, there, he’s Cregan Stark. He fought with Prince Aemon once, and the Dragonknight said he’d never faced a finer swordsman.” They were almost at the end now, and Bran felt a sadness creeping over him. “And there’s my grandfather, Lord Rickard, who was beheaded by Mad King Aerys. His daughter Lyanna and his son Brandon are in the tombs beside him. Not me, another Brandon, my father’s brother. They’re not supposed to have statues, that’s only for the lords and the kings, but my father loved them so much he had them done.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran列Jon、Rickard、Theon Hungry Wolf、两位Brandon、Rodrik、Torrhen、Cregan，最后讲祖父Rickard、Lyanna与叔父Brandon statues。
blocks.literary_note_zh: crypt tour把House identity建于conquest、loss、kneeling与memory；Bran能复述说明其lord education。
blocks.background_zh: **crypt statues：** 通常只为lords/kings雕像；Eddard因爱为siblings Lyanna、Brandon破例。

blocks.original_text: “The maid’s a fair one,” Osha said.

blocks.original_text: Bran saw eyes like green fire, a flash of teeth, fur as black as the pit around them. Maester Luwin yelled and threw up his hands. The torch went flying from his fingers, caromed off the stone face of Brandon Stark, and tumbled to the statue’s feet, the flames licking up his legs. In the drunken shifting torchlight, they saw Luwin struggling with the direwolf, beating at his muzzle with one hand while the jaws closed on the other.

blocks.original_text: And Summer came, shooting from the dimness behind them, a leaping shadow. He slammed into Shaggydog and knocked him back, and the two direwolves rolled over and over in a tangle of grey and black fur, snapping and biting at each other, while Maester Luwin struggled to his knees, his arm torn and bloody. Osha propped Bran up against Lord Rickard’s stone wolf as she hurried to assist the maester. In the light of the guttering torch, shadow wolves twenty feet tall fought on the wall and roof.

blocks.background_zh: **Rickon dream：** Rickon独立说同夜梦见Eddard并守在其empty tomb，使Luwin单纯wish-fulfillment解释承压。

Blood dripped down his arm where Shaggydog had shredded the wool of his sleeve and the flesh beneath. “Osha, the torch,” he said, biting through his pain, and she snatched it up before it went out. Soot stains blackened both legs of his uncle’s likeness. “That . . . that beast,” Luwin went on, “is supposed to be chained up in the kennels.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 受伤Luwin前所未有地不确定，命Osha捡torch，并说Shaggydog本应锁在kennels。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Luwin uncertain来自兄弟同梦与真实crypt行为叠合，而不仅是狼伤。

blocks.original_text: Maester Luwin’s turret was so cluttered that it seemed to Bran a wonder that he ever found anything. Tottering piles of books covered tables and chairs, rows of stoppered jars lined the shelves, candle stubs and puddles of dried wax dotted the furniture, the bronze Myrish lens tube sat on a tripod by the terrace door, star charts hung from the walls, shadow maps lay scattered among the rushes, papers, quills, and pots of inks were everywhere, and all of it was spotted with droppings from the ravens in the rafters. Their strident quorks drifted down from above as Osha washed and cleaned and bandaged the maester’s wounds, under Luwin’s terse instruction. “This is folly,” the small grey man said while she dabbed at the wolf bites with a stinging ointment. “I agree that it is odd that both you boys dreamed the same dream, yet when you stop to consider it, it’s only natural. You miss your lord father, and you know that he is a captive. Fear can fever a man’s mind and give him queer thoughts. Rickon is too young to comprehend—”
blocks.explanation_zh: cluttered tower充满books、jars、lens、charts、ink和raven droppings；Osha为Luwin清创，他以fear解释兄弟同梦。
blocks.literary_note_zh: fear can fever mind是可行心理解释，但same dream、crypt location和wolves反应使证据并未完全关闭。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: “I’m four now,” Rickon said. He was peeking through the lens tube at the gargoyles on the First Keep. The direwolves sat on opposite sides of the large round room, licking their wounds and gnawing on bones.

blocks.original_text: “And all this they did with magic,” Maester Luwin said, distracted. “I wish they were here now. A spell would heal my arm less painfully, and they could talk to Shaggydog and tell him not to bite.” He gave the big black wolf an angry glance out of the corner of his eye. “Take a lesson, Bran. The man who trusts in spells is dueling with a glass sword. As the children did. Here, let me show you something.” He stood abruptly, crossed the room, and returned with a green jar in his good hand. “Have a look at these,” he said as he pulled the stopper and shook out a handful of shiny black arrowheads.
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin讽刺magic若真有用可无痛治臂，又以glass sword比喻trust spells，并拿出black arrowheads。
blocks.literary_note_zh: glass sword比喻说magic看似锋利却易碎；随后真实obsidian也像glass，令比喻复杂化。
blocks.background_zh: **obsidian：** volcanic glass，可制极锋利但脆的arrowheads；dragonglass是常用别称。

blocks.original_text: Bran picked one up. “It’s made of glass.” Curious, Rickon drifted closer to peer over the table.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran说材质像glass，Rickon也靠近看。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dragonglass引出children、First Men、Pact和Andal conquest历史，Osha坚持old races仍在Wall以北。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: “Obsidian,” Maester Luwin insisted, holding out his wounded arm. “Forged in the fires of the gods, far below the earth. The children of the forest hunted with that, thousands of years ago. The children worked no metal. In place of mail, they wore long shirts of woven leaves and bound their legs in bark, so they seemed to melt into the wood. In place of swords, they carried blades of obsidian.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin坚持叫obsidian，说明children不冶metal，以leaves/bark和obsidian weapons生活。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dragonglass/obsidian是同一材料的文化命名差异；Luwin强调natural substance，Osha保留older vocabulary。

blocks.original_text: Maester Luwin tugged at his chain collar where it chafed against his neck. “They were people of the Dawn Age, the very first, before kings and kingdoms,” he said. “In those days, there were no castles or holdfasts, no cities, not so much as a market town to be found between here and the sea of Dorne. There were no men at all. Only the children of the forest dwelt in the lands we now call the Seven Kingdoms.
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin讲Dawn Age：men到来前，children是Seven Kingdoms土地最早居民。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Luwin叙述明确带maester historiography：where/how long no man can know标记证据界限。
blocks.background_zh: **children of the forest：** men到来前居民，信old gods，由greenseers刻weirwood faces；历史主要来自songs/tradition。

blocks.original_text: “They were a people dark and beautiful, small of stature, no taller than children even when grown to manhood. They lived in the depths of the wood, in caves and crannogs and secret tree towns. Slight as they were, the children were quick and graceful. Male and female hunted together, with weirwood bows and flying snares. Their gods were the gods of the forest, stream, and stone, the old gods whose names are secret. Their wise men were called greenseers, and carved strange faces in the weirwoods to keep watch on the woods. How long the children reigned here or where they came from, no man can know.
blocks.explanation_zh: children矮小灵巧、男女共同hunting，信奉old gods，greenseers刻weirwood faces；来源年代无人知道。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dragonglass引出children、First Men、Pact和Andal conquest历史，Osha坚持old races仍在Wall以北。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的设定；重点在Bran/Rickon共同dream、Luwin与Osha的知识分歧、direwolf行为及Winterfell继承责任。

blocks.original_text: “But some twelve thousand years ago, the First Men appeared from the east, crossing the Broken Arm of Dorne before it was broken. They came with bronze swords and great leathern shields, riding horses. No horse had ever been seen on this side of the narrow sea. No doubt the children were as frightened by the horses as the First Men were by the faces in the trees. As the First Men carved out holdfasts and farms, they cut down the faces and gave them to the fire. Horror-struck, the children went to war. The old songs say that the greenseers used dark magics to make the seas rise and sweep away the land, shattering the Arm, but it was too late to close the door. The wars went on until the earth ran red with blood of men and children both, but more children than men, for men were bigger and stronger, and wood and stone and obsidian make a poor match for bronze. Finally the wise of both races prevailed, and the chiefs and heroes of the First Men met the greenseers and wood dancers amidst the weirwood groves of a small island in the great lake called Gods Eye.
blocks.explanation_zh: 约十二千年前First Men跨Broken Arm而来，以bronze、horses、砍weirwoods引发战争；children可能施magic断Arm，最终双方在Gods Eye会盟。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 技术差造成asymmetric war；dark magic shattering Arm被old songs限定，不作确证。
blocks.background_zh: **First Men wars：** 约十二千年前bronze-using First Men进入，砍weirwoods引战，最终在Gods Eye议和。

blocks.original_text: “There they forged the Pact. The First Men were given the coastlands, the high plains and bright meadows, the mountains and bogs, but the deep woods were to remain forever the children’s, and no more weirwoods were to be put to the axe anywhere in the realm. So the gods might bear witness to the signing, every tree on the island was given a face, and afterward, the sacred order of green men was formed to keep watch over the Isle of Faces.
blocks.explanation_zh: Pact划分deep woods并禁止再砍weirwoods，green men守Isle of Faces。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pact把土地、weirwoods与witness绑定，old gods worship由政治和平传播。
blocks.background_zh: **Pact：** deep woods归children、禁止砍weirwood；green men守Isle of Faces。

blocks.original_text: “The Pact began four thousand years of friendship between men and children. In time, the First Men even put aside the gods they had brought with them, and took up the worship of the secret gods of the wood. The signing of the Pact ended the Dawn Age, and began the Age of Heroes.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pact开启四千年友好，First Men改信old gods，Dawn Age转入Age of Heroes。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dragonglass引出children、First Men、Pact和Andal conquest历史，Osha坚持old races仍在Wall以北。
blocks.background_zh: **Age of Heroes：** Pact后约四千年友好期，First Men逐渐采用old gods信仰。

blocks.original_text: Bran’s fist curled around the shiny black arrowhead. “But the children of the forest are all gone now, you said.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bran握紧dragonglass，指出Luwin说children如今全没了。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dragonglass引出children、First Men、Pact和Andal conquest历史，Osha坚持old races仍在Wall以北。

blocks.original_text: “Here, they are,” said Osha, as she bit off the end of the last bandage with her teeth. “North of the Wall, things are different. That’s where the children went, and the giants, and the other old races.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Osha不是说children everywhere可见，只说north of Wall情况不同；仍是个人证词。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, very well,” Luwin muttered. “So long as the kingdoms of the First Men held sway, the Pact endured, all through the Age of Heroes and the Long Night and the birth of the Seven Kingdoms, yet finally there came a time, many centuries later, when other peoples crossed the narrow sea.
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin勉强继续：Pact历经Age of Heroes、Long Night和Seven Kingdoms诞生，直至新族群渡海。

blocks.original_text: “The Andals were the first, a race of tall, fair-haired warriors who came with steel and fire and the seven-pointed star of the new gods painted on their chests. The wars lasted hundreds of years, but in the end the six southron kingdoms all fell before them. Only here, where the King in the North threw back every army that tried to cross the Neck, did the rule of the First Men endure. The Andals burnt out the weirwood groves, hacked down the faces, slaughtered the children where they found them, and everywhere proclaimed the triumph of the Seven over the old gods. So the children fled north—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Andals以steel、fire与Seven征服六个southern kingdoms，焚weirwoods、杀children；只有North挡在Neck后维持First Men统治，children逃向north。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Andal conquest解释North宗教文化差异，并把children迁徙方向推向Wall外。
blocks.background_zh: **Andals：** 携steel、Seven信仰征服南方六国；North在Neck后守住First Men文化。

blocks.original_text: Maester Luwin broke off, startled. When Shaggydog bounded to his feet and added his voice to his brother’s, dread clutched at Bran’s heart. “It’s coming,” he whispered, with the certainty of despair. He had known it since last night, he realized, since the crow had led him down into the crypts to say farewell. He had known it, but he had not believed. He had wanted Maester Luwin to be right. The crow, he thought, the three-eyed crow . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Shaggydog加入后Bran确定坏消息来了；三-eyed crow带他告别，他早知却希望Luwin正确。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bran说certainty of despair不是从letter得知，而是dream、howls与情绪累积后的确信。

blocks.original_text: Maester Luwin stared at the black bird as if it were a scorpion with feathers. He rose, slow as a sleepwalker, and moved to the window. When he whistled, the raven hopped onto his bandaged forearm. There was dried blood on its wings. “A hawk,” Luwin murmured, “perhaps an owl. Poor thing, a wonder it got through.” He took the letter from its leg.
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin像面对feathered scorpion般靠近，发现bird wings有dried blood，取下letter。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Luwin此前视raven作工具，如今像scorpion，说明rational observer也恐惧已知消息。

blocks.original_text: Maester Luwin looked up at them numbly, a small grey man with blood on the sleeve of his grey wool robe and tears in his bright grey eyes. “My lords,” he said to the sons, in a voice gone hoarse and shrunken, “we . . . we shall need to find a stonecarver who knew his likeness well . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Luwin含泪以my lords称兄弟，说需要找熟悉Eddard相貌的stonecarver。
blocks.literary_note_zh: stonecarver委婉确认Eddard死亡；不用dead一词，更符合Luwin面对children时的破碎表达。
blocks.background_zh: **stone likeness：** Stark dead在crypt中由stonecarver刻像；Luwin此言即准备Eddard memorial。
